_schema_note: 1.1 = test_results split into backend/frontend sub-objects (established W10); everything else mirrors the W9 1.0 shape.
wave: Wave B (the city comes alive: EM-115 auto-build city growth, EM-111 warm toon golden hour, EM-118 instanced foliage + props, EM-122 per-kind building meshes)

resolution.summary: QE gate for the 4-item Wave B batch on build/wave-b-city-comes-alive: EM-115 (deterministic city growth — BuildingParams.auto_build_per_round=10, advance_buildings() auto-advances every under_construction building per round, completion routed through the NEW shared _complete_construction helper so the agent build_step path and the work-crew reflex emit identical event kinds/payload keys; planned/unfunded + damaged stalls still rot to abandoned; auto=0 escape hatch restores pre-EM-115 behavior end-to-end), EM-111 (toon.ts cached MeshToonMaterial factory + 4-step warm ramp with a 0.45 luminance floor, vendored CC0 Poly Haven venice_sunset_1k.hdr in web/public/hdri/ recorded in ASSET_LICENSES.md, golden-hour sun/hemi/ambient/fog in CozyWorld per the art doc), EM-118 (pure deterministic foliageLayout.ts — 60 instanced trees in 3 variants with static LOD split, lamps/benches/fences/bushes/rocks/mushrooms, all hashUnit-seeded, place-center + slot-band + path-corridor clearance), EM-122 (operationalVariant + pure clamped healthTint in worldSpace.ts, 10 per-variant procedural sub-components in Structure.tsx, all materials via the shared toon cache, soot tint for damaged-but-operational bodies). Full gates re-run by QE: backend 352/352, web 235/235 across 29 files, vite build green (pre-existing >500kB chunk warning only). Adversarial probes all green: snapshot round-trip with an under_construction building restores progress/last_progress_tick and auto-build resumes to completion with exactly one building_operational; forage bonus activates after a restore+auto-complete; auto_build_per_round=0 on a RESTORED world reproduces old stall-rot; damaged buildings still abandon with auto on; negative auto values safely behave as disabled; healthTint survives malformed hex / #rgb / NaN / out-of-range health; operationalVariant handles empty and unicode kinds (-> generic). One real new gap found (prototype-key building kinds now crash the canvas instead of mis-painting — see issues), nothing blocking.

contract_conformance.notes: Verified criterion-by-criterion against contracts/wave-b.md by reading the diffs, not the agents' reports. EM-115: Building.to_dict() key set verified UNCHANGED vs contracts/world-model.md §Building (exact set asserted in test_building_to_dict_unchanged_by_em115; auto_build_per_round lives only in BuildingParams and never enters the dict — grep-verified); the completion helper is genuinely shared (action_build_step's inline completion block was REMOVED and replaced by the _complete_construction call, so the two paths cannot drift); building_operational payload keys identical by construction and asserted set-equal in tests; advance_buildings still returns list[dict] and loop.py is untouched this wave (git diff main...HEAD shows zero loop.py edits — the existing _advance_round_buildings flush at loop.py:842-868 iterates the returned list, setdefaults turn_id=None, and emits; probed live); actor_id=None on the reflex events matches the abandonment house pattern and events.schema.json allows null. EM-111: toon.ts cache keyed by the FULL param tuple (color lowercased, emissive, emissiveIntensity, transparent, opacity) — identical params return the identical instance, asserted in toon.test.ts incl. omitted-vs-explicit-default equivalence; ramp is 4 steps, NearestFilter min+mag, generateMipmaps=false, floor 0.45 (warm-never-black, asserted monotonic); every world3d component swept — zero meshStandardMaterial remains, meshBasicMaterial labels/rings kept as contracted, the single inline meshToonMaterial (NoticeBoard-style DoubleSide plane in Structure.tsx:226) is a documented per-mount allocation because side isn't a cache option; CozyWorld has the contract sun (#FFCF99 @ 2.2, castShadow), hemi #FFE9C2/#3A5A2A, SoftShadows, fog, Sky + Environment files=/hdri/venice_sunset_1k.hdr; HDRI is CC0 Poly Haven, recorded in ASSET_LICENSES.md, served from public/ (not bundled). EM-118: TREE_PLACE_CLEAR=10.5 satisfies the contract's '≥ ~10', tested with a literal >=10 assertion over every tree x every center; non-lamp props tested out of every path corridor (PATH_CLEAR), lamps tested BESIDE ribbons never on them; instance budget tested <400 per-part; zero Math.random (all hashUnit). EM-122: operationalVariant returns the exact contracted VariantKey set with ordered substring-trap keywords; healthTint is pure, clamped, identity at >=100, capped at MAX_SOOT_MIX, returns malformed input untouched. Docked one point for two literal deviations: (1) the contract says auto-completion emits 'the SAME structure_state_changed payloads' — the payload KEY SETS are identical (tested) but the `reason` value deliberately differs ('raised by the village work crew' vs 'completed'); intent (no drift, shared helper) is met, but a strict reading of 'same payloads' is not; (2) EM-118's near-prop placement reinterprets the contract's slot-band clearance against the prop's OWN place only (with a 3.7 neighbor floor) because a strict all-places rule is geometrically unsatisfiable in real procgen towns — the deviation is honestly documented in foliageLayout.ts's header and enforced by tests, but it is a reinterpretation, not the letter. Interim per-round progress events were made silent, which the contract explicitly prefers ('prefer that — but the completion events are mandatory'); completion events verified mandatory-and-once.

test_coverage.notes: Backend +9 tests in NEW backend/tests/test_wave_b_citygrowth.py covering every contract-mandated case: ceil(100/auto)-round completion with ZERO build_steps + silent interim rounds + last_progress_tick refresh, never-abandons-past-the-window, payload-key set equality vs the build_step path (a real cross-path assertion, not a tautology), function activation (forage +3 after auto-complete), planned/unfunded still abandons, auto=0 restores stall rot (incl. no tick refresh), build_step+auto composition clamped at 100 with exactly ONE building_operational, auto-overshoot clamp (auto=33), and the to_dict() exact-key-set guard. One pre-existing test (test_w7.py stall-rot) was modified ADDITIVELY only: it now sets auto_build_per_round=0 to keep pinning the path the contract deliberately changed — no assertion weakened (git diff: additions only). Web +44 tests: toon.test.ts 14 (ramp steps/filters/no-mipmaps/monotonic-warm-floor, cache identity incl. default-equivalence and case-insensitivity, distinct-params-distinct-instances, emissive/transparent honored), foliageLayout.test.ts 18 (determinism incl. call-order independence, tree count band 50-80, literal >=10 clearance, pairwise tree spacing, LOD split completeness, lamp/bench own-ring bounds, wild props >= BAND_MAX, path-corridor exclusion for every non-lamp item, lamps-beside-never-on ribbons, instance budget < 400, degenerate empty/single-place worlds), worldSpace.test.ts +12 (operationalVariant exact keys, emergent keyword mappings, case-insensitivity, substring-trap ordering, generic fallback; healthTint identity/clamp/purity/valid-hex/monotonic/soot-cap/malformed-untouched). Honest gaps: (a) no test feeds a prototype-member kind ('constructor'/'toString') through operationalVariant/buildingStyle — QE's live probe shows these return Object.prototype members and now crash (see issues); (b) no snapshot-round-trip test for a mid-construction building resuming auto-build (QE probed it green manually — worth one backend test); (c) negative auto_build_per_round is untested (probed: safely behaves as disabled via the auto > 0 guard); (d) Structure.tsx's per-variant geometry and the useFrame transform animations have no render-level tests (consistent with the repo's convention of testing pure modules, and the orchestrator browser gate covers the visual).

security.notes: Smallest security surface of any wave so far: no new endpoints, no new request shapes, no new persistence, no new agent-reachable text paths. Free-scale law verified by grep over the full backend diff (git diff main...HEAD -- backend/): the only matches for chat/llm/provider/completion tokens are docstrings and comments — zero new LLM/provider call sites; advance_buildings is a pure deterministic reflex. The one new external input class is the vendored binary asset: web/public/hdri/venice_sunset_1k.hdr (1.0 MiB, CC0, Poly Haven, recorded in ASSET_LICENSES.md) — served statically, parsed only by three.js's RGBELoader in the browser, never touched by the backend. Model-authored building `kind` strings already flowed to the renderer pre-wave; EM-122 adds two more pure functions on that path (operationalVariant/healthTint), both probed with unicode/empty/malformed input without crash — the surviving prototype-key robustness hole (see issues) is a crash/DoS-of-the-canvas class at worst, requires the model to name a building kind exactly 'constructor'/'toString'/etc., and never executes attacker code (native prototype members only, no __proto__ assignment). No secrets, no auth surface, no SQL, no file I/O added anywhere in the wave.

regression_risk.notes: Full backend 352/352 and web 235/235 with the new tests in place; vite build green. The wave's one INTENDED behavior change — funded (under_construction) projects can no longer rot to abandoned while auto-build is on — is contract-mandated, escape-hatched (auto=0 probed on both fresh and snapshot-restored worlds), and the only pre-existing test it touched was adapted additively. Snapshot/replay verified unchanged: Building.to_dict() byte-identical key set, last_progress_tick already serialized so a restored mid-construction building resumes auto-build seamlessly (probed: restore at 30% -> exactly one building_operational, function activates). loop.py needed zero edits — the existing flush consumes the same list[dict] shape. Renderer risk hunted specifically: every useFrame in world3d (CozyWorld camera, Structure bob/clock/smoke/progress-ring, Villager/Critter movement) mutates ONLY transforms — no shared cached material is ever written after construction, so the cache cannot cross-contaminate meshes; no per-frame allocations found. Known watch items, none blocking: (1) healthTint feeds toonMaterial, so each distinct (color, health-step) pair allocates one permanently-cached material — bounded (<=2 per health integer per style) and never disposed by design, but a long arson-heavy run accretes up to ~200 extra materials; (2) operationalVariant escalates the pre-existing prototype-key hole from cosmetic to canvas-crash (see issues); (3) the main JS chunk grew to 1948 kB (pre-existing >500kB warning class; the HDRI is NOT in the bundle); (4) structure_state_changed `reason` for auto-completions is a new string value ('raised by the village work crew') — payload-additive, no frontend switch on reason exists (grep-verified feed renders text/kind only).

frontend.command: cd web && npm run test -- --run (vitest run)
frontend.changed_existing: worldSpace.test.ts gained 12 EM-122 tests (operationalVariant + healthTint) beside the existing EM-130/131 suites — additions only, no existing assertion touched; test_w7.py (backend) gained one auto_build_per_round=0 line to keep pinning the contract-changed stall path

test_results.frontend_verification: cd web && npm run test -- --run — 235/235 across 29 files; npm run build — clean in 5.66s (pre-existing >500kB chunk warning only, main chunk now 1948 kB; the 1.0 MiB HDRI ships via public/, not the bundle)

issues.title: Prototype-member building kinds ('constructor', 'toString', ...) now CRASH the 3-D canvas instead of mis-painting — EM-122 escalates the known EM-130 hole
issues.detail: web/src/components/world3d/worldSpace.ts:228 (operationalVariant) and :159 (buildingStyle, pre-existing) index plain object literals, so a model-authored kind exactly matching an Object.prototype member returns a native function as the 'variant'/'style' (QE probe: operationalVariant('constructor') === Object.prototype.constructor). Pre-wave the consequence was cosmetic (meshStandardMaterial color={undefined} silently defaults). Post-wave, style.body===undefined reaches toonMaterial(undefined) -> cacheKey's color.toLowerCase() throws TypeError, and VARIANT_COMPONENTS[<function>] yields an undefined React component — either way the whole R3F canvas render crashes on ONE weird building. Probability is very low (kind must EXACTLY equal a prototype key; unicode/empty/junk kinds were probed safe -> 'generic'), the root is pre-existing and flagged MINOR in the Wave A report, and the fix is one line per lookup (Object.hasOwn guard or null-prototype map) — recommend fixing both lookups in the next wave rather than blocking this gate.

issues.title: structure_state_changed `reason` value differs between the two completion paths — keys identical, strict 'same payloads' reading not met
issues.detail: backend/petridish/engine/world.py:1243 passes reason='completed' from the agent build_step path while world.py:1417 passes 'raised by the village work crew' from the auto-build reflex (the shared _complete_construction helper parameterizes it). Payload KEY sets are identical by construction and asserted set-equal in test_auto_completion_payload_keys_match_build_step_completion_path; building_operational payloads are fully identical. The contract's 'emit the SAME structure_state_changed + building_operational events/payloads' is met in structure but not in the reason VALUE — arguably intended (the contract separately demands in-character work-crew feed flavor), and no consumer switches on reason (grep-verified), but recording it as a literal deviation.

issues.title: toonMaterial cache grows one permanent entry per distinct healthTint output — bounded but never evicted
issues.detail: web/src/components/world3d/Structure.tsx:935-936 calls toonMaterial(healthTint(style.body|roof, health)) — every distinct (color, health-integer) pair allocates a new MeshToonMaterial that lives in the module cache forever (toon.ts:102, deliberately never disposed). Bounded at <=101 health steps x 2 colors x ~12 styles (~2400 worst case, realistically tens), so not a leak in practice, and the cache is exactly what prevents per-frame allocation — but a quantize-health-to-steps (e.g. 10% buckets) in healthTint callers would cap it near-free. Also noted: cacheKey does not normalize 3-digit hex ('#fff' and '#ffffff' allocate two identical materials) — harmless duplication.

issues.title: Negative auto_build_per_round silently behaves as 0 (disabled) — sane but unvalidated and untested
issues.detail: backend/petridish/engine/world.py:1404 guards `auto > 0`, so a config of auto_build_per_round=-5 falls through to the stall-rot branch (QE probe: building abandons at its last manual progress, exactly like auto=0). This is the right behavior, but neither config/loader.py validates the field nor does any test pin it; a one-line test in test_wave_b_citygrowth.py would lock it in.

issues.title: No snapshot-round-trip test for mid-construction auto-build resume — QE probed it green manually
issues.detail: World.from_snapshot restores status/progress/last_progress_tick for an under_construction building, auto-build resumes and completes with exactly one building_operational, and the forage bonus activates afterward (QE REPL probe, all green — last_progress_tick was already in to_dict() pre-wave so nothing new needed serializing). But no automated test covers restore-then-auto-complete, and the fork endpoint (POST /api/runs/fork) is exactly the seam where this composition runs in production. Recommend one test in test_wave_b_citygrowth.py.

issues.title: Remaining observations (summarized; none behavioral defects)
issues.detail: EM-118: the near-prop slot-band clearance is enforced against the prop's OWN place only (NEAR ring 3.2-4.6 < SLOT_BASE_RADIUS 5.5) plus a 3.7 neighbor floor, because adjacent procgen places sit ~8.6 units apart and a strict all-places band rule is unsatisfiable — honestly documented in foliageLayout.ts:14-21 and pinned by tests; benches/lamps can therefore stand inside a NEIGHBOR's outer slot band in dense towns (structures there are rare since rings fill inner-first). EM-118: layoutTrees caps attempts at TREE_COUNT*30, so a pathological many-place world could yield <50 trees — the count-band test pins the real procgen world only. EM-111: the DoubleSide pennant plane in Structure.tsx:226 uses an inline (per-mount, not per-frame) meshToonMaterial because `side` isn't a cache option — one allocation per planned clocktower, negligible. EM-111: main chunk grew to 1948 kB (pre-existing warning class; drei Environment/SoftShadows pull weight; the HDRI itself is public/-served). EM-122: OPERATIONAL_LABEL_Y heights are per-variant constants — unknown kinds fall to 'generic' so the lookup is total. EM-115: a building damaged mid-construction (status='damaged') does NOT auto-build and still rots to abandoned (QE probe) — matches the contract's 'abandonment still applies to damaged stalls'.

gate_decision.reason: All four Wave B items verified against contracts/wave-b.md by direct code reading + live probes, and every gate is green: backend 352/352 ('352 passed, 1 warning in 27.53s'; +9 non-tautological tests in test_wave_b_citygrowth.py), web 235/235 across 29 files (+44 tests in toon.test.ts / foliageLayout.test.ts / worldSpace.test.ts), npm run build clean. The contract's hard invariants all hold: Building.to_dict() key set unchanged vs world-model.md with auto_build_per_round provably excluded; build_step and auto-build completions share one helper so building_operational payloads are identical (and structure_state_changed keys identical — only the reason VALUE differs, recorded as a MINOR literal deviation); loop.py untouched and the existing flush consumes advance_buildings()'s list[dict] unchanged; zero new LLM calls in the backend diff (free-scale law grep-verified); the toon material cache returns identical instances for identical params and no useFrame anywhere mutates a shared material; tree clearance >=10 and path-corridor exclusion are genuinely asserted, not just claimed; healthTint/operationalVariant survived malformed-hex/unicode/empty adversarial input. Adversarial probes (snapshot round-trip mid-construction, auto=0 on a restored world, damaged-stall rot with auto on, negative auto) all behaved correctly. The 6 recorded issues are MINOR — the one real new gap (prototype-key kinds escalating from mis-paint to canvas crash) needs an exact model-authored kind of 'constructor'/'toString' and is a one-line hasOwn fix for the next wave. GREEN to proceed to the ux-review + ledger gate.
